title: Repository Baseline, Go 1.26 Toolchain, Modules, and Reproduction · Guided Systems Studio
summary: A regional field-research team incrementally builds reproducible Go Pokedex repository baseline from original fixed PokéAPI fixtures while retaining prior Go, HTTP, Git, quality-gate, accessibility, security, testing, cancellation, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of reproducible Go Pokedex repository baseline while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, a…
objectives: Inspect revision, branch, worktree, ignored files, configuration, tests, fixtures, secrets, licenses, and recovery anchor before editing.
Record Go 1.26.5, GOOS, GOARCH, GOTOOLCHAIN, module mode, build tags, and supported platform matrix.
Choose module path, command and internal package boundaries, dependency direction, and import visibility before growth.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Pokedex Go Repository Sta…
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go team handles case 6068 during a narrow-terminal ranger workflow. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile Go toolchain, module, clean-build, target, and dependency evidence; browser code must remain deterministic and side-effect-free; then change one command, argument, EOF, or output-width condition and preserve the session contract. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile Go toolchain, module, clean-build, target, and dependency evidence; the fixed PokéAPI origin and fair-use traffic budget must remain enforced; then change one toolchain, target, artifact, migration, rollback, or restore condition and reconcile the release. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Run format, test, vet, race-capable tests, build, and a fixture smoke command from a clean compatible environment.

One precondition fails while output still looks plausible. What is the correct response?

Evidence target: Pokedex product engineering and Go Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Pokedex Go Clean Reproduc….

stimulus.title: Pokedex Go Clean Reproduction failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: A binary built from a dirty developer cache is reproducible evidence.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Run format, test, vet, race-capable tests, build, and a fixture smoke command from a clean compatible environment.

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Pokedex Go Clean Reproduction condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

Keep the plausible Pokedex Go Clean Reproduction output and hide the violated reproducible Go Pokedex repository baseline assumption instead of explaining the failure. That claim fails the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary for Pokedex Go Clean Reproduction.

For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Pokedex Go Clean Reproduction happy path, omit ownership, error, changed input, goroutine stop, and full-toolchain checks, then accept plausible output. This does not satisfy Pokedex Go Clean Reproduction for reproducible Go Pokedex repository baseline.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Run format, test, vet, race-capable tests, build, and a fixture smoke command from a clean compatible environment.
